schema.description: Supply a list of property IDs to filter properties in the response. Note that if a page doesn't have a property, it won't be included in the filtered response.
Whether to include meeting note transcripts. Defaults to false. When true, full transcripts are included; when false, a placeholder with the meeting note URL is shown instead.

sub_group_by.description: Secondary group-by configuration for sub-grouping within columns. Pass null to remove sub-grouping.

properties.description: Property visibility and display configuration on cards. Pass null to clear.

cover.description: Cover image configuration for cards. Pass null to clear.

cover_size.description: Size of the cover image on cards. Pass null to clear.

cover_aspect.description: Aspect ratio mode for cover images. "contain" fits the image, "cover" fills the area. Pass null to clear.

card_layout.description: Card layout mode. "list" shows full cards, "compact" shows condensed cards. Pass null to clear.

properties.description: Properties to display on each card.

card_layout.description: Card layout mode (list shows all properties, compact shows inline).

workspace_id.description: The ID of the bot's workspace.

workspace_name.description: The name of the bot's workspace.

type.description: Indicates this user is a bot.

bot.description: Details about the bot, when the `type` of the user is `bot`.

date_property_id.description: Property ID of the date property used to position items on the calendar.

properties.description: Property visibility and display configuration on calendar cards. Pass null to clear.

view_range.description: Default calendar range. "week" shows a week view, "month" shows a month view. Pass null to clear.

show_weekends.description: Whether to show weekend days. Pass null to clear.

date_property_id.description: Date property used to position items on the calendar - required.

properties.description: Properties to display on calendar event cards.

aggregator.description: The aggregation operator. "count" counts all rows and does not require a property_id. All other operators require a property_id.

property_id.description: The property to aggregate on. Required for all operators except "count".

aggregator.description: The aggregation operator. "count" counts all rows and does not require a property_id. All other operators require a property_id.

property_id.description: The property to aggregate on. Required for all operators except "count".

value.description: The y-axis value where the reference line is drawn.

label.description: Label displayed alongside the reference line.

dash_style.description: "Line style: \"solid\" for a continuous line, \"dash\" for a dashed line."

value.description: The y-axis value where the reference line is drawn.

label.description: Label displayed alongside the reference line.

dash_style.description: "Line style: \"solid\" for a continuous line, \"dash\" for a dashed line."

x_axis.description: X-axis grouping configuration for grouped data mode. Pass null to clear.

y_axis.description: Y-axis aggregation for grouped data mode. Pass null to clear.

x_axis_property_id.description: Property ID for x-axis values in results mode. Pass null to clear.

y_axis_property_id.description: Property ID for y-axis values in results mode. Pass null to clear.

value.description: Aggregation for number charts. Pass null to clear.

sort.description: Sort order for chart data. Pass null to clear.

color_theme.description: Color theme. Pass null to clear.

height.description: Chart height. Pass null to clear.

hide_empty_groups.description: Whether to hide groups with no data. Pass null to clear.

legend_position.description: Legend position. Pass null to clear.

show_data_labels.description: Whether to show data labels. Pass null to clear.

axis_labels.description: Which axis labels to show. Pass null to clear.

grid_lines.description: Which grid lines to show. Pass null to clear.

cumulative.description: Cumulative values (line only). Pass null to clear.

smooth_line.description: Smooth line curves (line only). Pass null to clear.

hide_line_fill_area.description: Hide area fill (line only). Pass null to clear.

group_style.description: Grouped/stacked bar display style. Pass null to clear.

y_axis_min.description: Custom y-axis minimum. Pass null to clear.

y_axis_max.description: Custom y-axis maximum. Pass null to clear.

donut_labels.description: Donut slice labels. Pass null to clear.

hide_title.description: Hide title (number only). Pass null to clear.

stack_by.description: Stack-by grouping for stacked/grouped bar charts. Pass null to clear.

reference_lines.description: Reference lines on the chart. Pass null to clear.

caption.description: Chart caption text. Pass null to clear.

color_by_value.description: Whether to color chart elements by their numeric value (gradient coloring). Pass null to clear.

chart_type.description: "The chart type: column (vertical bars), bar (horizontal bars), line, donut, or number (single value display)."

x_axis.description: X-axis grouping configuration for column/bar/line/donut charts using grouped data. Null when using results (raw property values) mode.

y_axis.description: Y-axis aggregation for column/bar/line/donut charts using grouped data. Null when using results mode.

x_axis_property_id.description: Property ID for the x-axis name values when using results (raw property values) mode.

y_axis_property_id.description: Property ID for the y-axis numeric values when using results (raw property values) mode.

hide_empty_groups.description: Whether to hide groups with no data on the x-axis.

legend_position.description: Legend display position. "off" hides the legend.

show_data_labels.description: Whether to show data value labels on chart elements.

cumulative.description: Whether to show cumulative values (line charts only).

smooth_line.description: Whether to use smooth curves (line charts only).

hide_line_fill_area.description: Whether to hide the shaded area under the line (line charts only).

group_style.description: How grouped/stacked bars are displayed. "normal" stacks values, "percent" normalizes to 100%, "side_by_side" places bars next to each other.

y_axis_min.description: Custom minimum value for the y-axis. Null clears the override.

y_axis_max.description: Custom maximum value for the y-axis. Null clears the override.

donut_labels.description: What to display on donut chart slices.

hide_title.description: Whether to hide the title label (number charts only).

stack_by.description: Stack-by grouping configuration for stacked/grouped bar charts (column/bar/line only). Null when not stacked.

reference_lines.description: Reference lines drawn on the chart. Null when no reference lines are configured.

caption.description: Text caption displayed below the chart. Null when no caption is shown.

color_by_value.description: Whether chart elements are colored by their numeric value (gradient coloring).

hide_empty_groups.description: Whether to hide groups that have no items.
Whether to hide groups that have no items.

width_ratio.description: Ratio between 0 and 1 of the width of this column relative to all columns in the list. If not provided, uses an equal width.
Ratio between 0 and 1 of the width of this column relative to all columns in the list. If not provided, uses an equal width.

filter.description: "Filter for the view. Uses the same format as the data source query filter (property filters, timestamp filters, or compound and/or filters). Simple property filters appear in the view's filter bar in the Notion UI. Select, status, and multi_select filter operators accept a single string or an array of strings to filter by multiple values (e.g. { \"does_not_equal\": [\"Done\", \"Archive\"] })."

items.description: Sort for the view. Can be a property sort (with property and direction) or timestamp sort (with timestamp and direction).

additionalProperties.description: "A property filter condition. Same shape as a property filter but without the \"property\" field (the hashmap key identifies the property). For example: { \"select\": { \"equals\": \"High\" } }."

quick_filters.description: Quick filters to pin in the view's filter bar. Keys are property names or IDs. Values are filter conditions (same shape as a property filter but without the property field). Each quick filter appears as a clickable pill above the view, independent of the advanced filter.

view_id.description: The ID of the collection view rendered by this widget.

width.description: Width of the widget in a 12-column grid (1-12). 12 means full width.

row_index.description: The 0-based index of the row this widget belongs to. Widgets in the same row share the same row_index.

attempt_number.description: The delivery attempt number (1-8) of the current event delivery.

api_version.description: The Notion API version that was used to render this webhook event's shape.

accessible_by.description: The users or bots who own the bot connection to the `integration_id` and have access to the webhook's `entity`. Only present for public integrations.

groups.description: The groups for the status property.

content.description: The actual text content of the text.

TextRichTextItemRequestText.description: If a rich text object's type value is `text`, then the corresponding text field contains an object including the text content and any inline link.

content.description: The actual text content of the text.

TextRichTextItemResponseText.description: If a rich text object's type value is `text`, then the corresponding text field contains an object including the text content and any inline link.

prefix.description: The prefix for the unique ID.

block_type.description: "The underlying block type that is not currently supported by the Public API. Example values include: form, button, drive."

oneOf.description: "A property filter condition. Same shape as a property filter but without the \"property\" field (the hashmap key identifies the property). For example: { \"select\": { \"equals\": \"High\" } }."

UpdateViewRequestQuickFilters.description: Quick filters for the view's filter bar. Keys are property names or IDs. Set a key to a filter condition to add/update that quick filter. Set a key to null to remove it. Pass null for the entire field to clear all quick filters. Unmentioned quick filters are preserved.

TextRichTextItemRequestTextLink.description: An object with information about any inline link in this text, if included.

TextRichTextItemResponseTextLink.description: An object with information about any inline link in this text, if included.

markdown.description: Page content as Notion-flavored Markdown. Mutually exclusive with content/children.

timezone.description: IANA timezone to use when resolving template variables like @now and @today (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authorizing user's timezone for public integrations, or UTC for internal integrations.

IANA timezone to use when resolving template variables like @now and @today (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authorizing user's timezone for public integrations, or UTC for internal integrations.

is_locked.description: Whether the page should be locked from editing in the Notion app UI. If not provided, the locked state will not be updated.

timezone.description: IANA timezone to use when resolving template variables like @now and @today (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authorizing user's timezone for public integrations, or UTC for internal integrations.

IANA timezone to use when resolving template variables like @now and @today (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authorizing user's timezone for public integrations, or UTC for internal integrations.

erase_content.description: Whether to erase all existing content from the page. When used with a template, the template content replaces the existing content. When used without a template, simply clears the page content.

parent.description: The new parent of the page.

content.description: The enhanced markdown content to insert into the page.

after.description: Selection of existing content to insert after, using the ellipsis format ("start text...end text"). Omit to append at the end of the page.

type.description: Insert the content at the start of the page.
Insert the content at the end of the page.

position.description: Explicit position for inserted content. Use {"type":"start"} to prepend or {"type":"end"} to append. Cannot be combined with after.

insert_content.description: Insert new content into the page.

content.description: The new enhanced markdown content to replace the matched range.

content_range.description: Selection of existing content to replace, using the ellipsis format ("start text...end text").

allow_deleting_content.description: Set to true to allow the operation to delete child pages or databases. Defaults to false.

replace_content_range.description: Replace a range of content in the page.

old_str.description: The existing content string to find and replace. Must exactly match the page content.

new_str.description: The new content string to replace old_str with.

replace_all_matches.description: If true, replaces all occurrences of old_str. If false (default), the operation fails if there are multiple matches.

content_updates.description: An array of search-and-replace operations, each with old_str (content to find) and new_str (replacement content).

allow_deleting_content.description: Set to true to allow the operation to delete child pages or databases. Defaults to false.

update_content.description: Update specific content using search-and-replace operations.

new_str.description: The new enhanced markdown content to replace the entire page content.

allow_deleting_content.description: Set to true to allow the operation to delete child pages or databases. Defaults to false.

replace_content.description: Replace the entire page content with new markdown.

width_ratio.description: Ratio between 0 and 1 of the width of this column relative to all columns in the list. If not provided, uses an equal width.

title.description: Title of data source as it appears in Notion.

name.description: The new name of the property.

properties.description: The property schema of the data source. The keys are property names or IDs, and the values are property configuration objects. Properties set to null will be removed.

in_trash.description: Whether the database should be moved to or from the trash. If not provided, the trash status will not be updated.

result_type.description: Optionally filter the results to only include pages or data sources. Regular, non-wiki databases only support page children. The default behavior is no result type filtering, in other words, returning both pages and data sources for wikis.

properties.description: Property schema of data source.

title.description: Title of data source as it appears in Notion.

is_default.description: Whether this template is the default template for the data source.

templates.description: Array of templates available in this data source.

has_more.description: Whether there are more templates available beyond this page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to use for the next page of results. Null if there are no more results.

parent.description: The parent page or workspace to move the database to. If not provided, the database will not be moved.

title.description: The updated title of the database, if any. If not provided, the title will not be updated.

description.description: The updated description of the database, if any. If not provided, the description will not be updated.

is_inline.description: Whether the database should be displayed inline in the parent page. If not provided, the inline status will not be updated.

in_trash.description: Whether the database should be moved to or from the trash. If not provided, the trash status will not be updated.

is_locked.description: Whether the database should be locked from editing in the Notion app UI. If not provided, the locked state will not be updated.

parent.description: The parent page or workspace where the database will be created.

is_inline.description: Whether the database should be displayed inline in the parent page. Defaults to false.

parent.description: The parent of the comment. This can be a page or a block.

rich_text.description: An array of rich text objects that represent the content of the comment.

parent.description: The parent of the comment. This can be a page or a block.

markdown.description: The content of the comment as a Markdown string. Comment Markdown supports inline formatting only (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, links), inline equations ($expression$), and mentions. Block-level Markdown such as fenced code blocks, headings, lists, tables, and blockquotes does not render as structured blocks in comments.

rich_text.description: An array of rich text objects that represent the content of the comment.

markdown.description: The content of the comment as a Markdown string. Comment Markdown supports inline formatting only (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, links), inline equations ($expression$), and mentions. Block-level Markdown such as fenced code blocks, headings, lists, tables, and blockquotes does not render as structured blocks in comments.

file_upload_id.description: ID of a FileUpload object that has the status `uploaded`.

attachments.description: An array of files to attach to the comment. Maximum of 3 allowed.

name.description: The custom display name to use

rich_text.description: An array of rich text objects that represent the updated content of the comment.

markdown.description: The updated content of the comment as a Markdown string. Comment Markdown supports inline formatting only (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, links), inline equations ($expression$), and mentions. Block-level Markdown such as fenced code blocks, headings, lists, tables, and blockquotes does not render as structured blocks in comments.

mode.description: How the file is being sent. Use `multi_part` for files larger than 20MB. Use `external_url` for files that are temporarily hosted publicly elsewhere. Default is `single_part`.

filename.description: Name of the file to be created. Required when `mode` is `multi_part`. Otherwise optional, and used to override the filename. Must include an extension, or have one inferred from the `content_type` parameter.

content_type.description: MIME type of the file to be created. Recommended when sending the file in multiple parts. Must match the content type of the file that's sent, and the extension of the `filename` parameter if any.

number_of_parts.description: When `mode` is `multi_part`, the number of parts you are uploading. This must match the number of parts as well as the final `part_number` you send.

external_url.description: When `mode` is `external_url`, provide the HTTPS URL of a publicly accessible file to import into your workspace.

file.description: The raw binary file contents to upload.

part_number.description: When uploading files greater than 20MB in parts, this is the current part number. Must be an integer between 1 and 1,000.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available.

next_cursor.description: The cursor to use for the next page of results, or null if there are no more results.

value.description: The text value to filter on.

anyOf.description: Text filter value for string_contains and similar operators.

value.description: The text value to filter on.

anyOf.description: Text filter value for string_contains and similar operators.

value.description: The text value to filter on.

anyOf.description: Text filter value for string_contains and similar operators.

items.description: Meeting notes filter node (combinator or property filter).

filters.description: Nested filters; each may be a combinator (and/or) or property filter.

filter.description: Optional filter for querying meeting notes. Supports combinator (and/or) and property filters on title, attendees, created_time, created_by, last_edited_time, last_edited_by.

sort.description: Optional sort order for the results. Each entry specifies a property name and direction.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50.

title.description: Title of the meeting note as rich text.

status.description: Current processing status of the meeting note transcription.

children.description: Block IDs for each tab (summary, notes, transcript).

start_time.description: ISO-8601 start time of the calendar event.

end_time.description: ISO-8601 end time of the calendar event.

calendar_event.description: Calendar event metadata associated with this meeting note.

start_time.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the recording started.

end_time.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the recording ended.

recording.description: Start and end times of the actual recording.

created_time.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this meeting note was created.

last_edited_time.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this meeting note was last edited.

has_children.description: Whether this block has child blocks.

in_trash.description: Whether this meeting note is in the trash.

has_more.description: Whether additional results exist beyond the returned limit.

ExamplePayload.description: Placeholder schema for path example refs

notionVersion.name: Notion-Version
notionVersion.description: The [API version](/reference/versioning) to use for this request. The latest version is `2026-03-11`.
